data.title: Dollar General tops Q3 estimates; updates FY outlook
data.description:
• Dollar General press release (DG): Q3 GAAP EPS of $1.28 beats by $0.35.
  • Revenue of $10.65B (+4.6% Y/Y) beats by $50M.
  • Same-Store Sales Increased 2.5%.
  • Year-to-Date Cash Flows From Operations Increased 28.4% to $2.8 Billion
  • Board of Directors Declares Quarterly Cash Dividend of $0.59 Per Share.
  • The effective income tax rate in the third quarter of 2025 was 23.6% compared to 23.2% in the third quarter of 2024.
  • As of October 31, 2025, total merchandise inventories, at cost, were $6.7 billion compared to $7.1 billion as of November 1, 2024, a decrease of 8.2% on an average per-store basis.
  • During the third quarter of 2025, the Company opened 196 new stores, remodeled 651 stores through Project Elevate and 524 stores through Project Renovate, and relocated 8 stores.

  • Fiscal Year 2025 Financial Guidance and Store Growth Outlook

    The Company is raising its financial expectations for the year, primarily to reflect its outperformance in the third quarter, as well as its improved outlook for the remainder of the year, while also taking into consideration the potential for uncertainty related to consumer behavior.

    The Company now expects the following for the fiscal year ending January 30, 2026 (“fiscal year 2025”):

    • Net sales growth of approximately 4.7% to 4.9%  (prior approximately 4.3% to 4.8%) vs. estimated growth of 4.62% Y/Y, compared to its previous expectation of approximately 4.3% to 4.8%
    • Same-store sales growth of approximately 2.5% to 2.7%, compared to its previous expectation of approximately 2.1% to 2.6%
    • Diluted EPS of approximately $6.30 to $6.50  (prior approximately $5.80 to $6.30) vs. consensus of $6.14, compared to its previous expectation of approximately $5.80 to $6.30
      • Diluted EPS guidance continues to assume an effective tax rate of approximately 23.5%

    The Company now expects capital expenditures, including those related to investments in the Company’s strategic initiatives, to be toward the lower end of the range of $1.3 billion to $1.4 billion.

    The Company’s financial guidance continues to assume no share repurchases in fiscal year 2025.

    The Company is also reiterating its plans to execute approximately 4,885 real estate projects in fiscal year 2025, including opening approximately 575 new stores in the U.S. and up to 15 new stores in Mexico, remodeling approximately 2,000 stores through Project Renovate, remodeling approximately 2,250 stores through Project Elevate, and relocating approximately 45 stores.

    Fiscal Year 2026 Store Growth Outlook

    For the fiscal year ending January 29, 2027 (“fiscal year 2026”), the Company plans to execute approximately 4,730 real estate projects, including opening approximately 450 new stores in the U.S. (as well as approximately 10 new stores in Mexico), fully remodeling approximately 2,000 stores through Project Renovate, remodeling approximately 2,250 stores through Project Elevate, and relocating approximately 20 stores.

data.title: Genesco misses Q3 estimates; lowers FY guidance
data.description:
• Genesco press release (GCO): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.79 misses by $0.07.
  • Revenue of $616.22M (+3.3% Y/Y) misses by $1.48M.
  • Comparable sales increased 3%, with stores up 5%
  • E-commerce sales represented 23% of retail sales.

  • The Company did not repurchase any shares during the third quarter of Fiscal 2026. The Company currently has $29.8 million remaining on its expanded share repurchase authorization announced in June 2023.

    Fiscal 2026 Outlook

    For Fiscal 2026, while the Company expects sales and operating income growth to last year, we are revising our outlook and lowering guidance:

    • Now expects total sales to be up about 2% (prior to be up 3% to 4%) vs. estimated growth of 3.51% Y/Y) and comparable sales to be up about 3% compared to Fiscal 2025, down from prior guidance for total sales to be up 3% to 4% and comparable sales up 4% to 5%
    • Now expects adjusted diluted earnings per share from continuing operations to be around $0.95 (prior $1.30 to $1.70) vs. consensus of $1.55), down from our prior expectation of $1.30 to $1.70
    • Guidance assumes no further share repurchases and a tax rate of 34% excluding the tax impact of OBBBA, up from prior guidance of 29%
  • Revises fourth quarter and full year outlook

data.title: Build-A-Bear Workshop reports mixed Q3 results; reaffirms FY outlook
data.description:
• Build-A-Bear Workshop press release (BBW): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.62 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $122.7M (+2.7% Y/Y) misses by $1.58M.
  • Net retail sales were $112.3 million and increased 2.5%.
  • Consolidated e-commerce demand (online orders fulfilled from either the Company’s warehouses or its stores) decreased 10.8%.
  • Commercial and International franchising segment revenues were a combined $10.4 million and increased 4.9%.
  • Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization (“EBITDA”) of $14.2 million, decreased by 14.6%, and represented 11.6% of total revenues.
  • The Company reaffirms its guidance for fiscal 2025.

  • 2025 Outlook

    The Company reaffirms its 2025 revenue, pre-tax income, and net-new-unit guidance.

    Specifically, the Company continues to expect:

    • Total revenues to grow on a mid-to-high-single-digit percentage basis (vs. estimated growth of 7.29% Y/Y).
    • Pre-tax income in the range of $62 million to $70 million, reflecting approximately $11 million of tariffs and related costs during the seven months from July through the end of the fiscal year
    • Net-new-unit growth of at least 60 experience locations through a combination of corporately-managed, partner-operated, and franchise business models

    In addition, for fiscal 2025, the Company continues to expect:

    • Capital expenditures in the range of $20 million to $25 million
    • Depreciation and amortization to approximate $16 million
    • The income tax rate to be in the range of 22% to 24%, excluding discrete items

data.title: Halliburton names new operations chief
data.description:
• Halliburton (HAL) appointed Shannon Slocum as executive VP and COO, effective January 1, 2026.
  • Reporting to Jeff Miller, Halliburton's chairman, president and CEO, Slocum will be responsible for the Company's global operations, as well as business development, health, safety and environment, and global technology.
  • Slocum joined Halliburton in 2005 as senior manager of innovation and marketing and has served in multiple technology, operations, product service, and business development roles.
  • Effective Jan. 1, 2026, Rami Yassine will succeed Slocum as president, Eastern Hemisphere, after serving as senior vice president, Middle East North Africa region.

data.title: Middleby to sell 51% stake in Residential Kitchen business at $885M
data.description:
The Middleby Corporation (MIDD) has announced that affiliates of 26North Partners have agreed to purchase a 51% controlling interest in Middleby’s Residential Kitchen business at a valuation of $885 million.

Middleby will own a 49% non-controlling interest in the new standalone joint venture. The transaction supports Middleby’s ongoing transformation into a focused commercial foodservice solutions provider.

“This transaction enables Middleby to partially monetize our Residential Kitchen business and provides significant upfront cash proceeds to invest in shareholder return initiatives, while at the same time enabling Middleby to participate in Residential Kitchen’s upside potential,” said Tim FitzGerald, Middleby CEO.

Residential Kitchen’s portfolio includes storied names, such as Viking, AGA, Rangemaster, La Cornue, Lynx, U-Line, Kamado Joe and Masterbuilt.

Source: Press Release

data.title: What investors need to know about Monster Beverage's Investor Day event
data.description:
Monster Beverage (MNST) held its Investor Day event this week in New York City.The management team highlighted strong performance in regions such as the Middle East and Africa region with launches like Monster Energy Landon Zero Sugar and Ultra Strawberry Dreams in other markets. The recent pricing adjustments were also noted, as well as the 2026 innovation pipeline that includes Monster Energy Strawberry Shots, Juice Monster flavors, and the female-focused Flirt brand.Crucially, Monster Beverage (MNST) management gave no indication of slowing topline momentum or any new material cost pressures that could cut into margins.  On Wall Street, Piper Sandler analyst Michael Lavery said the firm continues to believe Overweight-rated Monster Beverage (MSNT) is well-positioned for strong, sustainable top-line growth momentum. Monster's (MSNT) innovation efforts with zero-sugar offerings are seen paying off. "We believe upside momentum to its gross margins is sustainable, driven by modest pricing and product mix (primarily driven by gains for zero sugar, but also concentrate sales like its strategic brands (including its affordable energy brands)," wrote Lavery.  
 
Elsewhere, Bank of America reiterated its Buy rating on Monster Beverage (MNST) following the event, noting the U.S. energy drink category remains one of the healthiest segments in the beverage sector and that international expansion remains a key growth lever. BMO came away from the Investor Day event confident in MNST's growth potential and raised its price target to $73, although it sees valuation as full. BNP Paribas doubled down on its Underperform thesis on Monster (MNST) due to what it sees as stretched valuation and long-term U.S. market share pressure. 
 
Shares of Monster Beverage (MNST) are up 40% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: TD Bank raises dividend by 3% to CAD 1.08 dividend
data.description:
• TD Bank (TD) declares CAD 1.08/share quarterly dividend, 2.9% increase from prior dividend of CAD 1.05.
  • Forward yield 3.67%
  • Payable Jan. 31; for shareholders of record Jan. 9; ex-div Jan. 9.
  • See TD Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: BlackRock reportedly halts plan for infra fund targeting wealthy Europeans
data.description:
BlackRock (BLK) is said to have halted plans to launch an infrastructure fund targeting wealthy Europeans.

The world’s largest asset manager was planning to launch a fund with a minimum investment of €10,000 ($11,660) in the first quarter of this year, according to a September 2024 presentation seen by Bloomberg News. Around the same time, BlackRock (BLK) registered an open-ended infrastructure fund with regulators in Luxembourg.

It decided to pause the effort because the relatively few and large deals done by its Global Infrastructure Partners unit weren’t a good enough fit for the vehicle, which would’ve allowed quarterly redemptions and let investors access the same investments as insurers and pension funds, the report said, citing people familiar with the matter.

Instead, the company is for now focusing on offering GIP deals in traditional drawdown funds, whose typical investors include ultra-high-net-worth individuals rather than the mass affluent, the people said.

data.title: Hormel Foods reports mixed Q4 results; initiates FY 26 outlook
data.description:
• Hormel Foods press release (HRL): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.32 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $3.19B (+1.6% Y/Y) misses by $30M.
  • Operating margin of 0.1%; adjusted operating margin1 of 7.7%.
  • Cash flow from operations of $323 million.

  • In fiscal 2026, the Company expects:

    • Net sales in the range of $12.2 billion to $12.5 billion (vs. consensus of $12.14B); organic net sales1 growth of 1% to 4%
    • Operating income in the range of $0.96 billion to $1.03 billion
    • Adjusted operating income1 in the range of $1.06 billion to $1.12 billion, growth of 4% to 10%
    • Diluted earnings per share in the range of $1.29 to $1.39
    • Adjusted diluted earnings per share1 in the range of $1.43 to $1.51 (vs. consensus of $1.37), growth of 4% to 10%
     

    Fiscal 2026 Outlook

    Net Sales

    $12.2 - $12.5 billion

    Adj. Operating Income1

    $1.06 - $1.12 billion

    Adj. Diluted EPS1

    $1.43 - $1.51

    The Company's fiscal 2026 outlook assumes:

    • Net sales growth across each reporting segment, despite a pressured consumer environment
    • A modest improvement in most commodity markets during the second half of the year, compared to fiscal 2025, though still above historical levels
    • Strong brand investments, with advertising support above recent levels
    • Benefits from growth and efficiency programs, including the T&M initiative and a recently announced program to reduce administrative expenses
    • Continued earnings pressure in the first quarter and earnings growth expected for the remainder of the year
    • An effective tax rate in the range of 21.5% to 22.5%
    • Capital expenditures in the range of $260 million to $290 million
    • Depreciation and amortization expense of approximately $280 million

data.title: Nutex Health extends share repurchase plan
data.description:
• Nutex Health (NASDAQ:NUTX)  announced on Thursday the extension of the share repurchase plan through March 31, 2026, and it will go into effect on December 4, 2025.
  • The program, originally announced on August 14, 2025, authorized the repurchase of up to $25M of the company's common stock.
  • The purpose of the share repurchase program is to increase shareholder value and offset dilution from the issuance of additional shares.
  • Shares +1.53%.
